page_title: "burnham Provider"
description: |-
    A pure provider-defined function provider organised into eleven families: cel (celencode: build a CEL / Common Expression Language string from an HCL data tree mirroring the cel/expr/syntax.proto canonical AST, in a readable surface notation or the canonical field-name notation, mixable; celvalidate: report whether a CEL string is syntactically valid (bool); celformat: canonicalize and pretty-print a hand-written CEL string; celdecode: parse a CEL string back into the celencode data tree in a chosen notation, so celencode/celdecode round-trip; celevaluate: evaluate a standard CEL expression at plan time against variable bindings; the encode/validate/decode functions are syntax-only and dialect-neutral so they suit GCP IAM / Access Context Manager, Kubernetes CEL, and any other CEL sink, while celevaluate evaluates cel-go's standard library plus extensions), compression (base64zopfli — a tighter RFC 1952 gzip drop-in for base64gzip via Zopfli's iterative DEFLATE encoder; base64brotli — RFC 7932 Brotli; both pure-Go, CGO-free, and deterministic for plan stability), cryptography (HMAC, HKDF, PEM/X.509/CSR/ASN.1 inspection, and a deterministic signing pipeline — {ecdsa_p256,ed25519}_key_from_seed + x509_self_sign + pkcs7_sign — for byte-stable Terraform-driven CMS/PKCS#7 signing, ECDSA via RFC 6979 deterministic k and Ed25519 via naturally-deterministic PureEdDSA per RFC 8032 / RFC 8419), dataformat (round-trip encoders/decoders for CBOR, HCL, HuJSON, INI, KDL, MessagePack, NDJSON, plist, REG, VDF, .env, .properties, Apple .strings; encode-only for JSON, CSV, YAML — Terraform ships the matching decoders as builtins), encoding (hex and base64 byte codecs — RFC 4648 base64 with URL-safe / no-padding options and a lenient decoder, plus the hex decode Terraform core lacks), geographic (geohash, Plus codes), identifiers (deterministic UUIDv5/v7, Nano ID, petname), network (IPv4/IPv6/CIDR helpers, NAT64, NPTv6, plus pigeon throughput from RFC 1149/2549), numerics (statistics, math helpers, Pi via RFC 3091 PDGP backed by an embedded ⌊π × 10⁶⌋-digit DPD-packed table), text (Unicode normalize, slugify, Levenshtein, wrap, cowsay, QR ASCII), and transform (JMESPath, JSONPath, JSON Patch / Merge Patch). No resources, no data sources, no remote API calls — every function evaluates at plan time.
---

# burnham Provider

> *"Aim high in hope and work, remembering that a noble, logical diagram once recorded will never die."*
> — Daniel Burnham

A pure provider-defined function provider — structured data formats, compression (Zopfli gzip and Brotli), IP/CIDR arithmetic, query and patch over decoded values, deterministic identifiers, text manipulation, cryptography (HMAC / HKDF / X.509 / CSR / ASN.1 / deterministic CMS signing), geographic encoding, and a small numerics family. No resources, no data sources, no remote API calls — every function evaluates at plan time.

Burnham is organized into eleven families. Per-function reference for all families lives in the sidebar.

## Compression Functions

Compress a string and base64-encode the result. `base64zopfli` is a tighter, drop-in replacement for the built-in `base64gzip` (ordinary RFC 1952 gzip output via Zopfli's iterative DEFLATE encoder — `gunzip` on the consumer side is unchanged); `base64brotli` is RFC 7932 Brotli (~8–10% smaller on text, needs a `brotli` decompressor on the consumer side). Both are pure-Go and deterministic, so plans stay stable.

## Structured Data Functions

Encode/decode functions for formats Terraform's standard library doesn't cover natively, plus pretty-printed `jsonencode` for the formats it does.

| Format | Encode | Decode | Notes |
|--------|--------|--------|-------|
| JSON (pretty-printed) | `jsonencode` | — | Terraform has `jsondecode` built-in |
| HuJSON / JWCC | `hujsonencode` | `hujsondecode` | JSON with comments and trailing commas |
| NDJSON / JSON Lines | `ndjsonencode` | `ndjsondecode` | One JSON value per line, trailing newline |
| Apple Property List | `plistencode` | `plistdecode` | XML (with comments), binary, and OpenStep formats |
| INI | `iniencode` | `inidecode` | Standard `[section]` / `key = value` files |
| CSV | `csvencode` | — | Terraform has `csvdecode` built-in |
| YAML | `yamlencode` | — | Block style, literal scalars, comments |
| Windows .reg | `regencode` | `regdecode` | Registry Editor export with typed values |
| Valve VDF | `vdfencode` | `vdfdecode` | Steam/Source engine config format |
| KDL | `kdlencode` | `kdldecode` | Modern document language, v1 and v2 |
| MessagePack | `msgpackencode` | `msgpackdecode` | Binary; base64-wrapped on the HCL side |
| CBOR | `cborencode` | `cbordecode` | RFC 8949, Core Deterministic Encoding; base64-wrapped |
| dotenv (.env) | `dotenvencode` | `dotenvdecode` | godotenv flavor: `KEY=value`, quoting, `${VAR}` interpolation |
| Java .properties | `javapropertiesencode` | `javapropertiesdecode` | `=`/`:`/whitespace separators, line continuation, `\uXXXX` escapes |
| Apple .strings | `applestringsencode` | `applestringsdecode` | Localization files. UTF-8 / UTF-16 BOM auto-detect on decode |
| HCL (general) | `hclencode` | `hcldecode` | Attribute-only HCL documents |

Tagged-value constructors (`plistdata`, `plistdate`, `plistreal`, `regbinary`, `regdword`, `regexpandsz`, `regmulti`, `regqword`) are consumed inside the matching encoder's input map; see the README and per-function pages for details.

## Networking Functions

Pure IP and CIDR operations: set arithmetic, queries, NAT64 (RFC 6052), NPTv6 (RFC 6296), IP arithmetic, and IPAM helpers. Backed by [`go4.org/netipx`](https://pkg.go.dev/go4.org/netipx) and `net/netip`.

Common entry points:
- **Set ops:** `cidr_merge`, `cidr_subtract`, `cidr_intersect`, `cidr_find_free`
- **Query:** `ip_in_cidr`, `cidr_contains`, `cidrs_overlap_any`, `cidrs_are_disjoint`
- **Arithmetic:** `ip_add`, `ip_subtract`, `cidr_first_ip`, `cidr_last_ip`, `cidr_host_count`
- **NAT64 / NPTv6:** `nat64_synthesize`, `nat64_synthesize_cidrs`, `nat64_extract`, `nptv6_translate`
- **Throughput:** `pigeon_throughput` ([RFC 1149](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc1149) / [RFC 2549](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc2549) IP over Avian Carriers)
- **Notation:** `ip_idunno_encode`, `ip_idunno_decode` ([RFC 8771](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc8771) I-DUNNO)

## Query and Patch Functions

Pure functions for querying and patching decoded structures, so manifest overlays and field extractions stay in plain HCL instead of regex hacks or `templatefile()`-driven preprocessors.

- **Query:** `jmespath_query` (JMESPath), `jsonpath_query` ([RFC 9535](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9535.html) JSONPath)
- **Patch:** `json_patch` ([RFC 6902](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc6902)), `json_merge_patch` ([RFC 7396](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc7396))

## Numerics Functions

Pure mathematical functions: an exhaustively faithful implementation of [RFC 3091](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc3091) (Pi Digit Generation Protocol) plus a small set of statistics and math helpers that fill gaps in Terraform's built-ins.

- **π:** `pi_digit(n)`, `pi_digits(count)`. Backed by an embedded table of the first ⌊π × 10⁶⌋ = 3,141,592 digits, encoded as IEEE 754-2008 [Densely Packed Decimal](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Densely_packed_decimal) — constant-time lookup.
- **22/7:** `pi_approximate_digit(n)`, `pi_approximate_digits(count)`. Period-6 repeating cycle `"142857"`; unbounded `n`.
- **Statistics:** `mean`, `median`, `mode`, `percentile`, `variance`, `stddev` (population formulas; sample variance is `var · N / (N − 1)`).
- **Math helpers:** `clamp`, `mod_floor` (floor-modulo — sign of divisor, unlike Terraform's built-in `%`).

## Identifiers Functions

Pure functions that produce stable, plan-time identifiers from a seed input. Same inputs always produce the same output, so a plan does not churn on re-apply.

- **UUIDs:** `uuid_v5` ([RFC 9562 §5.5](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9562#name-uuid-version-5)), `uuid_v7` ([RFC 9562 §5.7](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9562#name-uuid-version-7)), `uuid_inspect`.
- **Short IDs:** `nanoid` (HMAC-SHA-256 in counter mode, 21-char URL-safe alphabet by default), `petname` (Heroku-style adjective-adverb-noun).

## Text Functions

Pure string manipulation and small text-rendering helpers, scoped to *not* duplicate Terraform's built-ins or [`northwood-labs/corefunc`](https://registry.terraform.io/providers/northwood-labs/corefunc/latest) (which owns case conversion).

- **Normalization / distance:** `unicode_normalize` (UAX #15), `levenshtein` (codepoint-aware), `slugify` (Unicode → ASCII transliteration), `wrap`.
- **Rendering:** `cowsay`, `qr_ascii` (half-block Unicode QR codes).

## Cryptography Functions

Keyed hashing, key derivation, certificate / CSR / ASN.1 inspection, and a deterministic ECDSA P-256 signing pipeline (`ecdsa_p256_key_from_seed` → `x509_self_sign` → `pkcs7_sign`) for byte-stable Terraform-driven signing workflows.

- **Symmetric:** `hmac` ([RFC 2104](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc2104)), `hkdf` ([RFC 5869](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc5869)).
- **Inspection:** `pem_decode` ([RFC 7468](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc7468)), `x509_inspect`, `x509_fingerprint`, `csr_inspect`, `asn1_decode` (BER/DER walk).
- **Deterministic signing (ECDSA P-256 via [RFC 6979](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc6979); Ed25519 via [RFC 8032](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc8032) PureEdDSA, naturally deterministic):** `ecdsa_p256_key_from_seed`, `ed25519_key_from_seed` (seed → PEM PKCS#8 key), `x509_self_sign` ([RFC 5280](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc5280) v3 self-signed cert, accepts either algorithm), `pkcs7_sign` ([RFC 5652](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc5652) CMS SignedData with no signed attributes, accepts either algorithm — ECDSA P-256 is the shape Apple's configuration-profile installer accepts; Ed25519 follows [RFC 8419](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc8419) for non-Apple consumers).

## Geographic Functions

Pure geocoding — `(latitude, longitude)` pairs into short alphanumeric strings and back.

- **Geohash:** `geohash_encode`, `geohash_decode` ([`mmcloughlin/geohash`](https://pkg.go.dev/github.com/mmcloughlin/geohash)).
- **Open Location Code / Plus codes:** `pluscode_encode`, `pluscode_decode` ([Google `open-location-code`](https://github.com/google/open-location-code)).

## Usage

```terraform
terraform {
  required_providers {
    burnham = {
      source = "keeleysam/burnham"
    }
  }
}

# No provider block needed — burnham is pure functions, no configuration.
```

Functions are called as `provider::burnham::<name>(...)`.

## Further reading

- **GitHub** — [keeleysam/terraform-provider-burnham](https://github.com/keeleysam/terraform-provider-burnham): full README, runnable examples, source.
- **Examples** — every function has a working snippet under [`examples/`](https://github.com/keeleysam/terraform-provider-burnham/tree/main/examples).
- **Per-function reference** — see the sidebar.
